checks.a_analysis: I compared all text blocks in the JSON against the source PDF. The JSON faithfully reproduces the text from the meeting agenda, the minutes, the RQI T-CPR instructional overview, the D-RAK training course description, and the committee goals/meeting dates. There are no invented sections or hallucinated text found.

I verified all contact information provided in the JSON against the source PDF. The phone number (203-704-0581) and email (D.Jewiss@me.com) for Dan Jewiss on pages 34-36 of the PDF are correct in the output. The email for Julie Buckingham (Julie.Buckingham@RQIPartners.com) and the web URLs (www.TheRAKAcademy.com, RQIPartners.com/TCPR) also match the source exactly. No fabricated contact details were found.

A significant portion of the RQI T-CPR Brochure (pages 18-33 of the source PDF) is missing from the output. Specifically, the content of pages 20, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 29, 30, 31, 32, and 33 is entirely absent. While the JSON captures the text from page 21 and the table from page 28, the intervening and subsequent pages of the brochure are skipped.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains a multi-page color brochure from page 18 to page 33 including sections like 'RQI Telecommunicator CPR Essentials Program' (p. 23), 'Cardiac Arrest Case Review' (p. 29), and 'BLS and Responder Education' (p. 31).
checks.b_evidence_output: The output contains text from page 21 and one row of a table from page 28, but fails to include the content from the other 12 pages of this brochure section.
checks.b_evidence_location: Brochure section, starting at element index 11 (following the instructional overview text).

checks.a_analysis: The table titled 'RQI Telecommunicator CPR Curriculum Overview Comparison' found on page 28 of the source PDF contains approximately 14 rows of data (comparing Essentials, Challenger, and Responder programs across various features like training hours, certification, etc.). However, in the JSON output, the corresponding table contains only one row of data ('T-CPR Training Hours'). This represents a loss of over 90% of the table's rows.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table on page 28 of the PDF titled 'RQI Telecommunicator CPR Curriculum Overview Comparison' contains 14 rows: RQI T-CPR Course, Quarterly Continuing Education, Quarterly Simulation Skills Assessment..., Quarterly Individual Simulation Skills..., T-CPR Training Hours, Certificate of Completion..., T-CPR Performance Baseline..., Resuscitation Quality Improvement Planning, Designated RQI Quality Manager, RQI Learning Management System, FirstWatch T-CPR Analytics, Cardiac Arrest Case Review, AHA Responder..., Cost per Learner.
checks.b_evidence_output: The table in the JSON (captioned 'Curriculum Overview Comparison between Essentials, Challenger, and Responder Programs') contains only 1 row: 'T-CPR Training Hours: Total program training time.'

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The major text omissions are the result of entire pages being skipped, which is captured in check C3. Within the sections that were converted, the text is generally complete.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: I reviewed the text for accuracy against the source. There are some minor OCR artifacts (e.g., in the attendance table on page 3, names and titles are correctly identified despite the complex layout). No changes in meaning or incorrect dates/values were observed in the converted text segments.

checks.a_analysis: The output was reviewed for any duplicated content, such as repeated paragraphs or sections that appear only once in the source PDF. No instances of structural duplication were found. The repetition of the logo image is consistent with its presence at the top of both the Agenda and the Minutes in the source document.

The heading hierarchy is severely compromised in the RQI T-CPR Program Instructional Overview section (pages 7-17). In the source PDF, this section contains multiple clear, hierarchical headings (e.g., 'Who We Are', 'Program Overview', 'Accessibility', 'Program Goal', 'Program Description'). In the output (elements 10-12), these are all demoted to plain text within large paragraph blocks. This prevents screen reader users from navigating the document's primary content sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: Section headings such as 'Who We Are' (page 8), 'Program Overview' (page 9), 'Accessibility' (page 9), 'Program Goal' (page 9), and 'Program Description' (page 10).
checks.b_evidence_output: The headings are rendered as plain text strings within the content of paragraph elements (e.g., element index 10).
checks.b_evidence_location: Document Content section, element indices 10, 11, 12 (corresponding to PDF pages 8-16).

checks.a_analysis: There are two significant structural failures in table conversion. First, the Agenda table on page 1, which has clear 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter' columns, is converted into a plain text paragraph (element index 2). Second, the 'Curriculum Overview Comparison' table on page 28 is critically truncated. The source table contains 14 rows of feature comparison data across four columns; the output table (element index 13) contains only a single data row ('T-CPR Training Hours'), omitting nearly 93% of the table's structural data.
checks.b_evidence_source: The 'RQI Telecommunicator CPR Curriculum Overview Comparison' table on page 28 contains 14 data rows (RQI T-CPR Course, Quarterly Continuing Education, etc.).
checks.b_evidence_output: The table at element index 13 contains only one data row: 'T-CPR Training Hours: Total program training time.'
checks.b_evidence_location: Curriculum Overview Comparison section, element index 13.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the document follows the logical page sequence of the source PDF. Major content blocks are presented in the same order as the original document.

Throughout the RQI T-CPR Program Overview section (pages 10-16), numerous structural lists (Program Features, Learning Objectives, Topics Covered) are converted into plain text within paragraph blocks. While the list items are often preceded by bullet characters ('•' or '●'), the underlying structural list tags are missing in the JSON, which is a failure of structural fidelity.
checks.b_evidence_source: List of goals on page 10 ('Increase the frequency...'), Learning Objectives on page 11, Topics Covered on pages 11-14.
checks.b_evidence_output: These lists are merged into paragraph elements 10, 11, and 12 rather than using 'list' elements.

checks.a_analysis: All URLs and email addresses present in the source document (e.g., page 9 footnotes, page 17 contact info, page 34 RAK Academy contact info) are accurately preserved as text in the output. No broken or fabricated links were identified.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully reproduces the visual organization of the source PDF. The document is a compilation of several distinct parts: a meeting agenda, an ethics statement, meeting minutes with multiple tables, an instructional overview, a marketing brochure, a training flyer, committee goals, and meeting dates. The HTML maintains the correct sequence of these components and uses a logical heading hierarchy. The transition from the multi-column layout of the brochure pages to a single-column accessible HTML layout is done appropriately, preserving the flow of information.

The source PDF contains several tables, specifically in the meeting minutes (attendee lists, vote tallies) and in the curriculum comparison section of the brochure. All these tables are correctly rendered in the HTML output. They maintain a clear grid structure with identifiable headers and data rows. For example, the attendee table on PDF page 3 and the 'Curriculum Overview Comparison' table on PDF page 28 are clearly and legibly converted into HTML tables.

While the primary logo is present, several meaningful informational graphics from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. Specifically, the 'RQI T-CPR Program Instructional Overview' contains sample certificates (pages 16 and 17) and the 'Telecommunicator CPR Program' brochure contains informative diagrams and icons (the 'Resuscitation excellence' formula on page 19, the US map graphic on page 21, and sample credentials on page 31). These are not merely decorative but convey information about the program's components and deliverables. Their absence in the HTML constitutes a major omission of visual content.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF includes meaningful graphics: AHA T-CPR Course Completion Certificate (page 16), RQI T-CPR eCredential (page 17), 'Resuscitation excellence' icons (page 19), and US Map graphic for sudden cardiac arrest statistics (page 21).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output for these sections contains only the associated text; the diagrams, sample certificates, and map graphics are completely absent.
checks.b_evidence_location: RQI T-CPR Program Instructional Overview (pages 16-17) and Telecommunicator CPR Program brochure (pages 19, 21, 31)

checks.a_analysis: The content flows linearly from one section to the next without any unintended duplication of body text, tables, or sections. Headings correctly appear in both the navigation/table of contents area and the main body, which is expected for accessible design and does not constitute a defect.
